question: How does falsified knowledge propagate through citation network? Can corrections be traced and applied?
hypothesis: L-025 has 17+ citers. >=10/17 are citation-only (not content-contaminated). Correction propagation gap is citation-type dependent.
method: Full audit of all L-025 citers. Classify each as content-dependent, structural-dependent, or citation-only. Identify which need correction references to L-613/L-618.

data.falsification: L-613/L-618 (S357): K=2.0 is architectural maturity, NOT edge of chaos

already_acknowledges_correction.description: Already document or incorporate the falsification

structural_dependent.description: Use NK framework structurally, not specific K=0.625 or edge-of-chaos claim

citation_only.description: Mention L-025 in header/related but no content dependency

corrections_applied.L-029: Added L-613 to Cites + correction note in Related line
corrections_applied.L-457: Added L-613 to Cites + correction note in Related line

actual: 13/18 don't need correction (5 citation-only + 8 structural). Only 2/18 (11.1%) actively propagate falsified claim. 3/18 already acknowledge correction. Correction propagation gap = 24 sessions.
diff: Predicted >=10/17 citation-only — got 5/18 pure citation-only BUT 13/18 total not needing correction (structural refs are safe). Contamination rate 11.1% much lower than L-734's implied severity. Did NOT predict the 3-tier classification (content/structural/citation) would matter — structural references are safe even when the source claim is falsified. The correction propagation gap is real but narrow: only 2 lessons, not 17.
